category_title: Children with autism who exhibit disruptive or avoidant behaviors (shouting, random clicking) during difficult reading tasks
minimal_context: The student is working through an online reading comprehension episode on the computer and needs to answer a question about the passage.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please read the short passage on the computer and answer the question at the end.

STUDENT: I don't know these words.

TUTOR: Just try your best to figure it out and pick an answer.

STUDENT: (Clicks an answer quickly) Next.

TUTOR: That is not the correct answer. You need to read it again.

STUDENT: (Shouts) No! Finished! (Clicks rapidly on any answer on the screen to move on)

options.A: Responds to the student during the activity by saying, 'Another question already? No way! I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going.'
options.B: Places a written cue card depicting the steps in front of the student and prompts them by pointing to the correct step they need to do.
options.C: Pats the student on the shoulder, smiles, and asks them to do another question on the computer during the session.
options.D: Grants full agency during the activity by telling the student, 'I don't care, just pick out answers that you would probably like.'
